{{Infobox book
| name = Reaper
| slug = cradle-10-reaper
| precedes = [[Dreadgod (novel)|Dreadgod]]
| follows = [[Bloodline (novel)|Bloodline]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-10-reaper.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2021
| isbn = 9781959001133
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 486
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The novel begins with historical reflections on [[Ozmanthus Arelius]], a legendary genius and founder of [[House Arelius]] who eventually became the [[Judge of Destruction]] known as Ozriel. In the present, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] explore the [[Sword Sage]]'s void space, discovering [[labyrinth]] records while [[Eithan]] establishes the [[Sect of Twin Stars]] to provide security for refugees. As [[Lindon]] works to help [[Mercy]] recover from spiritual injuries and recruit [[Ziel]] for an upcoming exploration, the Blackflame Emperor{{!}}Blackflame Emperor Naru Huan arrives to pay respects to [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]]. During this time, the [[Monarch]] [[Reigan Shen]] begins manipulating the power structure of [[Sacred Valley]] by infiltrating labyrinths to alter the distribution of Cores.

A major shift occurs when a massive wave of [[hunger aura]] hits [[Sacred Valley]], signaling that the [[Dreadgods]]' suppression field has changed. [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]], [[Eithan]], [[Mercy]], and [[Ziel]] form a team to enter labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth using a mummified hand as a key. As they navigate the shifting, space-manipulating facility—which bears the crest of Ozriel—they face deadly [[Tomb Hydras]] and hungry ghouls{{!}}ghouls. During their journey, [[Lindon]] attempts to repair [[Dross]]'s spirit, resulting in the spirit evolving into a functional but emotionless entity. [[Eithan]] reveals his past as an advisor to the [[Arelius family]] and introduces the [[Soulforge]], a high-level [[Soulsmithing]] facility.

The group's exploration leads them to chambers containing the memories of [[Ozmanthus Arelius]] and the birth tanks of the [[Dreadgods]]. Amidst these discoveries, they are confronted by the [[Monarch]] [[Reigan Shen]]. A high-stakes battle ensues, forcing [[Eithan]] to intervene and eventually leading to a pursuit deeper into the maze. During this period, [[Mercy]] achieves an [[Overlady]] revelation through training, and [[Lindon]] crafts powerful new weapons, [[Genesis]] and [[Midnight]]. While navigating labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth, [[Lindon]] learns from a manifestation of the [[Slumbering Wraith]] that [[Dreadgods]] are sustained by [[Monarchs]] and can only be made mortal by destroying their anchors.

The conflict escalates on a cosmic scale as the [[Abidan Judges]], led by [[Suriel]], engage the apocalyptic [[Mad King]] in the stars of Fathom. Simultaneously, inside labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth, [[Lindon]] faces [[Reigan Shen]] once more. After surviving a brutal onslaught by drawing [[soulfire]] from [[natural treasures]], [[Lindon]] uses his Authority{{!}}authority over labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth to banish the [[Monarch]]. As [[Reigan Shen]] attempts to achieve immortality by fusing with a core binding, [[Lindon]] asserts his control over labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth's power layers. 

The story reaches its climax when [[Eithan]] reveals his true identity: he is Ozriel, a powerful being from the [[Court]] of Seven. He engages [[the Mad King]] in a cosmic battle, ultimately forcing the antagonist into the Void to protect the world. Following this confrontation, seven members of the [[Abidan]] descend to arrest [[Eithan]] for his actions and his role in [[the Mad King]]'s arrival. As the [[Abidan]] depart, [[Suriel]] remains to repair the world, leaving [[Lindon]] and his companions to face a new reality where the true identities of their mentors are known and the leaders of [[Cradle]] react to the vacuum left by [[Eithan]]'s departure.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|As ash falls from the sky, [[Ozmanthus Arelius]] reflects on his failure to protect his descendants from a devastating attack. Despite his loss, he decides to leave behind a small orb containing a fragment of his power as a final message to his family. This record serves as the documentation of his attempt to communicate one last time.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] arrives at [[Ziel]]'s cloud fortress with [[Elder Whisper]], a five-tailed snowfox who reveals that the [[Dreadgods]] can be made mortal by destroying an anchor located at the bottom of labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth. While [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] prepare to seek this truth, the [[Monarch]] [[Reigan Shen]] explores an ancient laboratory within labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth with the intent of seizing control of the [[Dreadgods]]. Meanwhile, [[Akura Malice]] and [[Northstrider]] discuss the shifting tides of [[fate]] and the growing threat posed by [[Shen]]'s manipulations.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The chapter begins with a personal record detailing the life of Ozmanthus Arelius{{!}}Ozmanthus, a legendary genius who sought the mythical city of [[Arelia]] and eventually founded [[House Arelius]]. In the present, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] explore the [[Sword Sage]]'s void space, where [[Yerin]] finds emotional closure among her master's belongings and they discover a dream tablets{{!}}dream tablet containing [[labyrinth]] records. Meanwhile, in the [[Abidan]] headquarters, [[Suriel]] broadcasts an urgent call for the missing Ozriel while contemplating how to coordinate the individualistic [[Judges]] to combat the advancing [[Vroshir]].}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] establishes the [[Sect of Twin Stars]] as a way to provide security and a future for refugees in [[Refuge]], using [[Lindon]] as an enigmatic figurehead. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] helps [[Mercy]] recover from her spiritual injuries by entering her soul's inner space and successfully extracting her from a nightmare realm. Finally, [[Lindon]] approaches [[Ziel]] to recruit him for their upcoming exploration of labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth, asserting that anyone who fights alongside them is part of his team.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|As the [[Blackflame Emperor]], [[Naru Huan]], arrives with a massive fleet to visit [[Lindon]] and his companions, [[Lindon]] struggles with a tense disagreement with his father regarding prosthetic eyes. To welcome [[the Emperor]], [[Eithan]] and [[Yerin]] perform a spectacular display of [[madra]] that dwarfs The Empire{{!}}the Empire's own arrival ceremony. The meeting concludes with [[Naru Huan]] showing immense respect to [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]], eventually venting his frustration with [[Eithan]] by delivering a powerful kick that sends the [[Archlord]] flying.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] discovers that [[Orthos]] has shrunk to the size of a small turtle following his soulfire transformation, though his spirit remains strong. While traveling on a cloudship, [[Lindon]] visits [[Jai Long]] and [[Kelsa]] to offer training, providing them with technical advice and guidance on their advancement. [[Lindon]] specifically instructs [[Jai Long]] to seek the [[Underlord revelation]] by reflecting on his original motivation for practicing the [[sacred arts]].}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] attempts to repair [[Dross]]'s damaged spirit in his new [[Soulsmith foundry]], but he and [[Seisha]] realize that any intervention could unpredictably reshape [[Dross]]'s personality or kill him. During a long journey to [[Serpent's Grave]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] discuss slowing down to live more normally while [[Eithan]] provides guidance on their divergent paths toward [[Monarch]] and [[Sage]] advancement. Meanwhile, the [[Lion Monarch]] [[Reigan Shen]] battles a natural spirit to claim the [[Storm Core]] in a [[labyrinth]], and the [[Sage]] and [[Herald of Redmoon Hall]] plot to use [[Yerin Arelius]] for their research once she returns to [[Sacred Valley]].}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|While [[Cassias]] and [[Eithan]] share a contemplative night in the city, [[Reigan Shen]] secretly infiltrates labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth to return stolen Cores to their original locations, effectively altering the power structure of [[Sacred Valley]]. [[Eithan]] experiences a disturbing premonition and rushes to investigate with [[Lindon]], fearing a shift in [[fate]] or Authority{{!}}authority. Upon arriving at the valley, they witness the suppression field's power source change and are struck by a massive, overwhelming wave of [[hunger aura]].}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Akura Malice]] uses her [[World of Night]] technique to perceive the future, only to be suddenly confronted by the presence of the four [[Dreadgods]]. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] recovers from a wave of power and joins [[Eithan]], [[Yerin]], [[Mercy]], and [[Ziel]] to form a team to enter labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth while its suppression field is down. During their preparations, [[Lindon]] uses dream madra to stabilize [[Dross]], but the spirit returns with a functional yet emotionless and passionless personality.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The chapter begins with a historical record detailing how [[Ozmanthus Arelius]], unable to join the [[Phoenix Division]], forged a peerless scythe and became the [[Judge of Destruction]] known as Ozriel. In the present, [[Lindon]] and his companions enter a shifting [[labyrinth]] in [[Sacred Valley]] using a mummified hand as a key, discovering that the facility bears the crest of Ozriel. As they navigate hunger-[[madra]] attacks and realize labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth's connection to the Arelius family{{!}}Arelius clan, a massive confrontation brews outside between [[Malice]]'s forces and the The Eight-Man Empire{{!}}Eight-Man Empire.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] and his companions explore a room filled with broken [[Soulsmith hammers]] before navigating a shifting [[labyrinth]] that uses space manipulation to trap them. After discovering a warning about a guardian called the [[Tomb Hydra]], they enter a tunnel marked with crossed swords. Simultaneously, [[Jai Long]] and his companions are conscripted by the [[Blackflame Empire]] and transported via portal back to [[Sacred Valley]] for combat.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|After defeating a [[Tomb Hydra]] and harvesting an advanced death-madra{{!}}death madra [[binding]] from its corpse, [[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]] are separated from the rest of the party by the shifting [[labyrinth]]. While [[Yerin]]'s group struggles against a new wave of hungry ghouls{{!}}ghouls and another Sandborn Hydra{{!}}Hydra, [[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]] navigate lethal traps to reach a chamber filled with significant historical artifacts. There, [[Eithan]] reveals insights into his past as an advisor to the [[Arelius family]] and warns that a powerful [[Monarch]] may be present within labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin]] successfully defends her companions against a [[Tomb Hydra]] and its subsequent arrival, eventually killing the beast with a powerful strike to prove she can protect them. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]] wait out a room of deadly constructs, during which [[Lindon]] studies the Sandborn Hydra{{!}}Hydra's unique [[binding]] and [[Eithan]] reveals a portable, high-level [[Soulsmithing]] facility called the [[Soulforge]]. The chapter concludes with a historical record detailing Ozriel's failed attempt to establish the 'Reaper{{!}}Reapers' division within the [[Abidan]].}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin]] leads her companions through a dangerous tunnel to escape a massive, flesh-like entity composed of multiple [[Heralds]]. After narrowly escaping the creature's death-madra{{!}}death madra, the group discovers a chamber belonging to [[Ozmanthus Arelius]], filled with invaluable [[dream tablets]] containing memories of the first Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch. As they attempt to study these legacies and plan their next move, a sudden, overwhelming presence signals the arrival of [[Reigan Shen]].}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Suriel]] decides to intervene in Sector Eleven{{!}}Sector 11 despite the lack of strategic necessity, leading her into a confrontation with [[the Mad King]] within Way{{!}}the Way. As she struggles against the [[Vroshir]]'s overwhelming assault, the other [[Judges]] of the [[Abidan Court]] arrive to support her. The chapter concludes with all seven [[Judges]] manifesting together on the world of Fathom to face [[the Mad King]] and his forces in a massive battle.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] and his companions confront the [[Monarch]] [[Reigan Shen]], engaging him in a high-stakes battle where they utilize various constructs and techniques. Despite their coordinated assault, the [[Monarch]]'s overwhelming power causes significant injuries to [[Mercy]] and pushes the group to their limits. The chapter concludes as [[Eithan]] intervenes with a powerful strike, shifting the focus of the conflict toward a direct confrontation between himself and the [[Monarch]].}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|After a fierce battle between [[Eithan]] and [[Reigan Shen]], [[Lindon]] successfully rescues [[Yerin]] from a water technique, though the group is left physically and spiritually exhausted. [[Reigan Shen]] retreats into the depths of labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth to pursue [[Tiberian]]'s dream, prompting [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]], and [[Eithan]] to resolve to follow him deeper into the maze. During the aftermath, [[Dross]] undergoes a permanent and unsettling evolution into a more powerful spirit, while the group prepares to face the [[Monarch]]'s ambitions.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|After recovering from their encounter with [[Reigan Shen]], [[Mercy]]'s companions prepare her for a training session in labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth using a hunger Ruler technique that manifests an echo of her mother, [[Akura Malice]]. Guided by [[Yerin]]'s encouragement to embrace her own identity rather than her mother's cruelty, [[Mercy]] successfully achieves an [[Overlady]] revelation. As the training concludes and the echo vanishes, [[Eithan]] notes that labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth is drawing the group deeper into its depths.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|After [[Jai Long]] successfully defeats an enemy squad and begins a slow, unexpected advancement to [[Underlord]], [[Lindon]] and his companions navigate a labyrinthine palace to find the birth tanks of the [[Dreadgods]]. Using the [[Soulforge]], [[Lindon]] crafts two powerful new weapons: a hammer named [[Genesis]] and a spear named [[Midnight]]. The group's progress is halted when labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth shifts, trapping them in a room containing the corrupted birthplace of [[Subject One]].}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Trapped in a sealed room by labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth, [[Lindon]] uses power lent by [[Orthos]] to break through the floor, allowing [[Orthos]] to escape via a spatial transfer script. As the group navigates further through labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth, they are forced to fight waves of hungry ghouls{{!}}ghouls and powerful combat echoes manifested by [[Subject One]]. After [[Ziel]] successfully defeats a formidable [[Archlord]]-level echo, an exit to the surface opens, revealing that a massive war involving [[Monarchs]] and [[House Shen]] is occurring above.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|After [[Yerin]] uses her immense power to force labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth into a deal, [[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]] descend into a chamber containing Ozriel's ruined [[Soulsmith inheritance]]. While [[Lindon]] mourns the lost knowledge, they are confronted by a powerful echo of [[Ozmanthus Arelius]] before [[Lindon]] is separated and faces the [[Slumbering Wraith]]. Meanwhile, outside labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth, [[Orthos]] and [[Little Blue]] encounter [[Redmoon Hall]] emissaries who intend to protect [[Lindon]]'s group, only for [[Yerin]] and [[Eithan]] to emerge from the gate.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|In the stars of Fathom, the seven [[Judges]] of the [[Abidan Court]] engage in a cosmic battle against [[the Mad King]] and his armies, with [[Suriel]] struggling to maintain reality as [[the Mad King]] attempts to tear open a portal to the Void. To prevent further destruction, [[Suriel]] allows [[the Mad King]] to retreat into the Void, though she realizes he still intends to destroy [[Cradle]]. Meanwhile, in a labyrinthine chamber, [[Lindon]] uses a hunger echo to speak with the manifestation of the [[Slumbering Wraith]], learning that [[Dreadgods]] are sustained by the presence of [[Monarchs]] and must be defeated by removing those [[Monarchs]] from the world. The chapter concludes as [[Reigan Shen]] arrives to confront [[Lindon]].}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|After [[Reigan Shen]] attempts to trick [[Lindon]] into a soul-oath{{!}}soul oath of fealty, the [[Monarch]] attacks him with various portals and powerful weapons. [[Lindon]] survives a brutal onslaught by drawing [[soulfire]] from scattered [[natural treasures]], triggering an advancement that restores his strength. Ultimately, [[Lindon]] uses his Authority{{!}}authority over labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth to shift space and banish [[Reigan Shen]] from the chamber, providing a temporary reprieve.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin]] and [[Eithan]] encounter [[Redmoon Hall]] emissaries who offer an audience with the [[Sage of Red Faith]], leading [[Yerin]] to agree to meet him after ensuring her people's safety. Meanwhile, [[Jai Long]] successfully reunites with his battered squad and finds them protected by [[Yerin]] and [[Eithan]]. In separate developments, [[Reigan Shen]] attempts to fuse with the [[Slumbering Wraith]]'s core binding to achieve immortality, while several [[Dreadgods]] experience profound shifts in consciousness and power.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Ozriel, seeking to escape his burdens and the [[Court]] of Seven, battles the [[Vroshir]] intruder [[Daruman]] in [[Iteration 216]] and successfully seizes an [[Origin Shroud]] to hide his existence. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] asserts his Authority{{!}}authority over labyrinth{{!}}the labyrinth by claiming its various layers of power and using Suriel's glass bead{{!}}Suriel's marble to secure his position. Following the battle, [[Akura Malice]] forces [[Lindon]] and his companions to swear a soul-oath{{!}}soul oath regarding the truth of the [[Dreadgods]], while [[Lindon]] notices that the flame within Suriel's glass bead{{!}}Suriel's marble has drastically diminished before finally going out.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|As an apocalyptic figure known as [[the Mad King]] begins destroying the world and extinguishing the stars, [[Eithan]] reveals his true identity as Ozriel, a powerful being from the [[Court]] of Seven. He engages [[the Mad King]] in a cosmic battle, using his Authority{{!}}authority to reclaim a scythe and eventually forcing the antagonist into the Void. Following the fight, [[Eithan]] explains his true name to a shocked [[Lindon]] and his companions before preparing to depart.}}
* '''Chapter 27:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|After [[Eithan]] stops a cosmic attack, seven members of the [[Abidan]] descend to arrest him for his actions and the arrival of [[the Mad King]]. [[Suriel]] remains behind to repair the damage to the world, revealing to [[Lindon]] that [[Eithan]] was attempting to raise a new [[Reaper]]. As the [[Abidan]] depart, the various leaders and monarchs of [[Cradle]] realize [[Eithan]]'s true identity and begin to react to his students being left behind.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Dross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Reigan Shen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ziel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Subject One]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Orthos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Little Blue]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ozriel (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Malice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ozmanthus Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Suriel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Mad King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Tiberian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Makiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Gadrael]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Silent King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Northstrider]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Wandering Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Red Faith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Kelsa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Tomb Hydra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Jai Long]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Oth’kimeth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Elder Whisper]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Naru Huan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Weeping Dragon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Herald of Redmoon Hall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Telariel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Emriss Silentborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Daruman]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Void Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sword Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Akura Malice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Naru Saeya]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Zakariel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|man in the bone armor}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Overlord (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Seisha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Jaran]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Presence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Hound]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Akura Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Blackflame Emperor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Suu]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Fury]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sage of Calling Storms]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Tiberian Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ruby]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Fingerling]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Durandiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Emperor (Cradle)]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sacred Valley]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Abidan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[labyrinth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Akura clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Blackflame Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Vroshir]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Windfall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Court of Seven (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Cradle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sector Eleven]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Haven]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Arelius family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Serpent's Grave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Redmoon Hall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[House Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|western labyrinth}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Dawn Sky Palace]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Mount Samara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Nethergate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Moongrave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[World of Night]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sanctum]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Judges]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ninecloud Court]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Eight-Man Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Desolate Wilds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Silverlords]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Heralds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Wolf]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Golds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Phoenix Division]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[hunger madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[hunger aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[soulfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|death-madra}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Hollow Domain]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Empty Palm]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Soulsmithing]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Striker technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Burning Cloak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[black dragon's breath]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Pure Storm Baptism]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[dream aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Soul Cloak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Dream of Darkness]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Forged madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ruler technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Goldsign]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Blood aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|dream madra}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Strings of Shadow]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Archlord soulfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Hunger aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|hunger}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Soulforge]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|launcher construct}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[dream tablets]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Storm Core]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[void key]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Thousand-Mile Cloud]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Penance]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Blood Core]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|goldsteel-plated tongs}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|hunger arm}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Netherclaw]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Moonlight Bridge]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Silent Core]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Titan Core]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Midnight]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Dreadgods]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Void Icon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Bleeding Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Herald]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Dreadgod]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Slumbering Wraith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Blackflame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Archlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Fate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Void (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[pure madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lowgold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Reaper]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Path of the White Fox]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Skysworn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Truegold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[binding]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Soulsmiths]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Uncrowned King Tournament]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL26049630W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
